zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud FitzJohn於大約1237年出生在英國波藍德波 白 桑德斯時，她的父親John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland30歲，母親Isabel le Bigod28歲。她於大約1260年與Gerard de Furnival在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1301年4月16日在英國維斯特莫藍德沙普聖 瑪麗 馬格達萊也斯 艾比享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: FitzJohn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name John , ‘son of John’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Fidgeon
meanings: 1  English: (i) variant of   Vivian  .  Compare  Videan  . (ii) variant of   Fitzjohn  .
2 Irish: reduced variant of Mag Guagáin, see McGuigan .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud FitzJohn was born about 1237, in Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, England, her father, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, was 30 and her mother, Isabel le Bigod, was 28. She married Gerard de Furnival about 1260, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 16 April 1301, in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud FitzJohn est née environ 1237, à Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Isabel le Bigod, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Gerard de Furnival environ 1260, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 avril 1301, à St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud FitzJohn um 1237 in Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland 30 und ihre Mutter Isabel le Bigod 28. Sie hat um 1260 in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom Gerard de Furnival geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. April 1301 im Alter von 65 Jahren in St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England und wurde in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud FitzJohn è nata il circa 1237 nel Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, England, sua padre, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Isabel le Bigod, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Gerard de Furnival circa 1260, nel Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 aprile 1301, nel St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, England, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolta a Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Elmley Castle, Worcester, England: イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストル
ja.lifeSummary: Maud FitzJohnが約1237年にイギリスクウンバーランドブルグ ビー サンズで生まれた時，父John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Irelandは30歳，母Isabel le Bigodは28歳であった。約1260年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアでGerard de Furnivalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1301年4月16日にイギリスウエストモーランドシャップスト マリー マグダリーネ'ス アビーで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスバークシュアイアレアディンググレイフリアルズ チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Grey Friars, Worcs, England: 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 리딩 그리프리아르스 처르치
ko.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: 영국 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Maud FitzJohn(이)가 대략 1237년 영국 쿠벌랜드 버그 비 샌드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel le Bigod(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1260년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르에서 Gerard de Furnival(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1301년 4월 16일 영국 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 리딩 그리프리아르스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud FitzJohn nasceu aproximadamente 1237, em Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Isabel le Bigod, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Gerard de Furnival aproximadamente 1260, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de abril de 1301, em St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Grey Friars, Worcs, England: грифрярс чурч, ридинг, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud FitzJohn родилась около 1237 г. в местности бург би сэндс, кумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel le Bigod, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerard de Furnival около 1260 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 апреля 1301 г. в местности ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности грифрярс чурч, ридинг, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud FitzJohn nació aproximadamente 1237, en Burgh by Sands, Cumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, John FitzGeoffrey Lord of Shere and Justiciar of Ireland, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Isabel le Bigod, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Gerard de Furnival aproximadamente 1260, en Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de abril de 1301, en St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmorland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
